name: Parsley

book_claims.claim: Contains high levels of apigenin which inhibits proliferation of tumour cells in cell culture
book_claims.mechanism: Flavone apigenin directly inhibits tumour cell proliferation

supplementary_research.finding: Apigenin suppresses cancer cell proliferation through multiple mechanisms: cell cycle arrest, induction of apoptosis, and inhibition of angiogenesis. Effective against prostate, breast, lung, and colon cancer cell lines at physiologically achievable concentrations.

Apigenin exhibits anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting NF-kB and COX-2 pathways. Also shows neuroprotective effects in animal models of Alzheimer's disease through reduction of oxidative stress and neuroinflammation.

Systematic review found parsley is one of the richest dietary sources of apigenin. Also contains myristicin, which shows chemoprotective activity in animal models by inducing glutathione S-transferase activity in the liver.

culinary_pairings.source: Persillade (garlic + parsley) is the classic French finishing condiment
Foundation of tabbouleh; classic in Italian tomato sauces
Salsa verde, chimichurri, and herb-oil dressings
Tabbouleh: parsley is the main ingredient, not a garnish
Classic herb for court-bouillon and fish en papillote
Essential in falafel, hummus garnish, and bean salads

seasonality: Available year-round. Peak freshness in spring and autumn. Grows easily in pots or garden beds. Flat-leaf (Italian) parsley has more flavour than curly varieties.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Relatively low oral bioavailability but regular dietary intake achieves meaningful tissue concentrations. Enhanced by fat co-consumption.
One of the richest food sources. Fat-soluble; pair with olive oil for best absorption.
Higher than oranges per gram. Degrades with heat; add fresh at end of cooking.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Variable (concentrated in seeds and essential oil)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Chemoprotective compound that induces liver detoxification enzymes (glutathione S-transferase).

synergies.description: Persillade tradition; parsley's vitamin C enhances iron absorption from garlic-containing dishes
Both enhance non-heme iron absorption; combined in gremolata and Middle Eastern salads
